rationale: The citing paper uses Van Bruggen et al. (2002) in its measurement procedure, not just as general background. In the data section, the authors explicitly state that they aggregate multiple informant responses using the confidence-based and response-data-based weighted mean procedures detailed by Van Bruggen et al. They also cite this work in the discussion as methodological guidance for handling distributed DMU evaluations. This shows a concrete role in constructing key variables for the empirical model.
relation_description: The citing study applies Van Bruggen et al.'s multiple-informant aggregation methods to combine respondent ratings for vendor performance and satisfaction in its business-market SPC analysis. The cited paper supplies the practical weighting and aggregation approach used in the citing paper's data construction.
